explanations.2: This line sets the default rule-checking order: first deny requests, then check allow rules—so allows can override denies.
explanations.3: This blocks all visitors by default, like a closed door.
explanations.4: This opens the door only for the specific IP address, allowing it through even though everything else is blocked.
explanations.7: This is the Apache 2.4 way to say 'block everyone'—the newer syntax replaced the older Order/Deny/Allow rules.
explanations.8: This is the Apache 2.4 way to say 'but let this specific IP address through'—it's the modern equivalent of line 4.
